root_text.an10.216:1.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.216:1.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.216:2.2: Kammassakā, bhikkhave, sattā kammadāyādā kammayonī kammabandhū kammapaṭisaraṇā, yaṁ kammaṁ karonti—
root_text.an10.216:2.3: kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādā bhavanti.
root_text.an10.216:3.1: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātī hoti luddo lohitapāṇi hatapahate niviṭṭho, adayāpanno sabbapāṇabhūtesu.
root_text.an10.216:3.2: So saṁsappati kāyena, saṁsappati vācāya, saṁsappati manasā.
root_text.an10.216:3.3: Tassa jimhaṁ kāyakammaṁ hoti, jimhaṁ vacīkammaṁ, jimhaṁ manokammaṁ, jimhā gati, jimhupapatti.
root_text.an10.216:4.1: Jimhagatikassa kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, jimhupapattikassa dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññataraṁ gatiṁ vadāmi—
root_text.an10.216:4.2: ye vā ekantadukkhā nirayā yā vā saṁsappajātikā tiracchānayoni.
root_text.an10.216:4.3: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, saṁsappajātikā tiracchānayoni?
root_text.an10.216:4.4: Ahi vicchikā satapadī nakulā biḷārā mūsikā ulūkā, ye vā panaññepi keci tiracchānayonikā sattā manusse disvā saṁsappanti.
root_text.an10.216:4.5: Iti kho, bhikkhave, bhūtā bhūtassa upapatti hoti.
root_text.an10.216:5.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco adinnādāyī hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.216:5.12: So saṁsappati kāyena, saṁsappati vācāya, saṁsappati manasā.
root_text.an10.216:5.13: Tassa jimhaṁ kāyakammaṁ hoti, jimhaṁ vacīkammaṁ, jimhaṁ manokammaṁ, jimhā gati, jimhupapatti.
root_text.an10.216:6.1: Jimhagatikassa kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, jimhupapattikassa dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññataraṁ gatiṁ vadāmi—
root_text.an10.216:6.2: ye vā ekantadukkhā nirayā yā vā saṁsappajātikā tiracchānayoni.
root_text.an10.216:6.3: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, saṁsappajātikā tiracchānayoni?
root_text.an10.216:6.4: Ahi vicchikā satapadī nakulā biḷārā mūsikā ulūkā, ye vā panaññepi keci tiracchānayonikā sattā manusse disvā saṁsappanti.
root_text.an10.216:6.5: Iti kho, bhikkhave, bhūtā bhūtassa upapatti hoti,
root_text.an10.216:6.9: Kammassakā, bhikkhave, sattā kammadāyādā kammayonī kammabandhū kammapaṭisaraṇā, yaṁ kammaṁ karonti—
root_text.an10.216:6.10: kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādā bhavanti.
root_text.an10.216:7.1: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho, lajjī dayāpanno sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharati.
root_text.an10.216:7.2: So na saṁsappati kāyena, na saṁsappati vācāya, na saṁsappati manasā.
root_text.an10.216:7.3: Tassa uju kāyakammaṁ hoti, uju vacīkammaṁ, uju manokammaṁ, uju gati, ujupapatti.
root_text.an10.216:8.1: Ujugatikassa kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, ujupapattikassa dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññataraṁ gatiṁ vadāmi—
root_text.an10.216:8.2: ye vā ekantasukhā saggā yāni vā pana tāni uccākulāni khattiyamahāsālakulāni vā brāhmaṇamahāsālakulāni vā gahapatimahāsālakulāni vā aḍḍhāni mahaddhanāni mahābhogāni pahūtajātarūparajatāni pahūtavittūpakaraṇāni pahūtadhanadhaññāni.
root_text.an10.216:8.3: Iti kho, bhikkhave, bhūtā bhūtassa upapatti hoti.
root_text.an10.216:9.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.216:9.4: pisuṇaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti …
root_text.an10.216:9.5: pharusaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti …
root_text.an10.216:9.11: ye imañca lokaṁ parañca lokaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedentī’ti.
root_text.an10.216:9.12: So na saṁsappati kāyena, na saṁsappati vācāya, na saṁsappati manasā.
root_text.an10.216:9.13: Tassa uju kāyakammaṁ hoti, uju vacīkammaṁ, uju manokammaṁ, uju gati, ujupapatti.
root_text.an10.216:10.1: Ujugatikassa kho pana ahaṁ, bhikkhave, ujupapattikassa dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññataraṁ gatiṁ vadāmi—
root_text.an10.216:10.2: ye vā ekantasukhā saggā yāni vā pana tāni uccākulāni khattiyamahāsālakulāni vā brāhmaṇamahāsālakulāni vā gahapatimahāsālakulāni vā aḍḍhāni mahaddhanāni mahābhogāni pahūtajātarūparajatāni pahūtavittūpakaraṇāni pahūtadhanadhaññāni.
root_text.an10.216:10.3: Iti kho, bhikkhave, bhūtā bhūtassa upapatti hoti.
root_text.an10.216:11.1: Kammassakā, bhikkhave, sattā kammadāyādā kammayonī kammabandhū kammapaṭisaraṇā, yaṁ kammaṁ karonti—
root_text.an10.216:11.2: kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā—tassa dāyādā bhavanti.
root_text.an10.216:11.3: Ayaṁ kho so, bhikkhave, saṁsappanīyapariyāyo dhammapariyāyo”ti.

translation_text.an10.216:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you an exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures.
translation_text.an10.216:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.216:2.1: “What is the exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures?
translation_text.an10.216:2.2: Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge.
translation_text.an10.216:2.3: They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad.
translation_text.an10.216:3.1: Take a certain someone who kills living creatures. They’re violent, bloody-handed, a hardened killer, merciless to living beings.
translation_text.an10.216:3.2: They’re creepy in body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an10.216:3.3: Doing crooked deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth are crooked.
translation_text.an10.216:4.1: Someone whose destiny and rebirth is crooked is reborn in one of two places, I say:
translation_text.an10.216:4.2: in an exclusively painful hell, or among the species of creepy animals.
translation_text.an10.216:4.3: And what are the species of creepy animals?
translation_text.an10.216:4.4: Snakes, scorpions, centipedes, mongooses, cats, mice, owls, or whatever other species of animal that creep away when they see humans.
translation_text.an10.216:4.5: This is how a being is born from a being.
translation_text.an10.216:4.6: For your deeds determine your rebirth,
translation_text.an10.216:4.7: and when you’re reborn contacts strike you.
translation_text.an10.216:4.8: This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds.
translation_text.an10.216:5.12: They’re creepy in body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an10.216:5.13: Doing crooked deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth are crooked.
translation_text.an10.216:6.1: Someone whose destiny and rebirth is crooked is reborn in one of two places, I say:
translation_text.an10.216:6.2: in an exclusively painful hell, or among the species of creepy animals.
translation_text.an10.216:6.3: And what are the species of creepy animals?
translation_text.an10.216:6.4: Snakes, scorpions, centipedes, mongooses, cats, mice, owls, or whatever other species of animal that creep away when they see humans.
translation_text.an10.216:6.5: This is how a being is born from a being.
translation_text.an10.216:6.6: For your deeds determine your rebirth,
translation_text.an10.216:6.7: and when you’re reborn contacts strike you.
translation_text.an10.216:6.8: This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds.
translation_text.an10.216:6.9: Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge.
translation_text.an10.216:6.10: They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad.
translation_text.an10.216:7.1: Take a certain someone who gives up killing living creatures. They renounce the rod and the sword. They’re scrupulous and kind, living full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an10.216:7.2: They’re not creepy in body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an10.216:7.3: Doing virtuous deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth is virtuous.
translation_text.an10.216:8.1: Someone whose destiny and rebirth is virtuous is reborn in one of two places, I say:
translation_text.an10.216:8.2: in a heaven of perfect happiness, or in an eminent well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain.
translation_text.an10.216:8.3: This is how a being is born from a being.
translation_text.an10.216:8.4: For your deeds determine your rebirth,
translation_text.an10.216:8.5: and when you’re reborn contacts strike you.
translation_text.an10.216:8.6: This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds.
translation_text.an10.216:9.1: Take someone else who gives up stealing …
translation_text.an10.216:9.12: They’re not creepy in body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an10.216:9.13: Doing virtuous deeds by way of body, speech, and mind, their destiny and rebirth is virtuous.
translation_text.an10.216:10.1: Someone whose destiny and rebirth is virtuous is reborn in one of two places, I say:
translation_text.an10.216:10.2: in a heaven of perfect happiness, or in an eminent well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain.
translation_text.an10.216:10.3: This is how a being is born from a being.
translation_text.an10.216:10.4: For your deeds determine your rebirth,
translation_text.an10.216:10.5: and when you’re reborn contacts strike you.
translation_text.an10.216:10.6: This is why I say that sentient beings are heirs to their deeds.
translation_text.an10.216:11.1: Sentient beings are the owners of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge.
translation_text.an10.216:11.2: They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad.
translation_text.an10.216:11.3: This is the exposition of the teaching on creepy creatures.”
